description: Fix the configuration reference page that shows a command returning a truncated secret instead of the full secret, causing auth failures for users who follow it.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Fix truncated secret command in configuration.mdx
userStories.description: As a user reading the Configuration reference, I want the secret retrieval command to return the full secret so that I can use it in my MCP client config without auth failures.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx line 144 uses 'opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret' instead of 'opentabs config show --json | jq -r .secret'
The explanatory text on line 147 is updated to mention --show-secret flag
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/configuration.mdx, lines 140-147.

Current code:
```
Retrieve it for MCP client configuration:

```bash
opentabs config show --json | jq -r .secret
```

This works because `opentabs config show` merges the secret from auth.json into its output for convenience. The source of truth is always auth.json.
```

The problem: without --show-secret, 'opentabs config show --json' returns a truncated secret like '8452...c823' (tested and confirmed). Users piping this into their MCP client config would get an auth failure.

Fix: Change the command to 'opentabs config show --json --show-secret | jq -r .secret' and update the explanatory text to mention that --show-secret is needed to output the full secret value. Other docs pages (quick-start.mdx line 48, install/index.mdx line 40, troubleshooting.mdx line 106, mcp-server.mdx line 599) already use the correct command with --show-secret.
